opportunities.title: [OSS] LuvLyrics/LuvLyricsApp: [Feature] Persist lyrics scan queue in SQLite so jobs survive app restarts
opportunities.description:
# Persist lyrics scan queue in SQLite so jobs survive app restarts

## Product context
LuvLyrics has a lyrics scan pipeline that discovers and stores lyrics for songs. Today this pipeline is memory-first, which means pending scan jobs disappear on app close, crash, or OS process kill. This creates user confusion and repeated work.

## Problem summary
- Queue state currently lives in runtime memory (Zustand state only).
- Pending/failed jobs are not durable.
- Contributors cannot rely on queue lifecycle across sessions.

## Goal
Introduce durable queue persistence so scan jobs can be resumed af
opportunities.llm_fit_note: ⚡ URGENT — [STRONG] Directly aligns with full-stack React and Zustand expertise, plus SQLite integration fits mobile/iOS background. | wins: ios_shipping, a11y_wcag, fullstack_taskflow

opportunities.title: [OSS] LuvLyrics/LuvLyricsApp: [Feature] Add one-tap lyrics edit flow from WebView copy result
opportunities.description:
# One-tap lyrics edit flow from WebView copy result

## Context
Manual search currently requires extra steps between copying lyrics and editing/applying them.

## Goal
Streamline this flow so users can jump directly into edit with prefilled content.

## References
- src/screens/LyricsWebViewScreen.tsx
- src/screens/AddEditLyricsScreen.tsx
- navigation typing modules

## Requirements
- After copy/extract success, show Edit now action.
- Navigate with payload (song context + lyric text).
- Preserve safe cancel path.

## Implementation plan
1. Add action button after copy success state.
2. Extend
opportunities.llm_fit_note: ⚡ URGENT — [STRONG] Direct React + TypeScript feature work on a real app with clear UX goals and implementation plan | wins: rubios_perf, a11y_wcag, complex_ui

opportunities.title: [OSS] LuvLyrics/LuvLyricsApp: [Feature] Cache last searched lyrics page per song for offline fallback
opportunities.description:
# Cache last searched lyrics page/content per song for offline fallback

## Problem context
Manual WebView lyrics search is valuable, but currently network dependent. Contributors need an offline fallback path.

## Goal
Cache last successful manual lyrics retrieval per song and surface it when online fetch is unavailable.

## Reference modules
- src/screens/LyricsWebViewScreen.tsx
- storage layer (src/store/* or DB helper)

## Implementation details
1. On successful copy/extract, save cache payload keyed by song ID.
2. Cache payload should include:
   - source URL
   - extracted lyrics text
opportunities.llm_fit_note: ⚡ URGENT — [STRONG] Directly aligns with full-stack React and local storage caching experience. | wins: rubios_perf, a11y_wcag, fullstack_taskflow

opportunities.title: [OSS] LuvLyrics/LuvLyricsApp: [Medium] Add provider smoke-test script with reproducible fixtures
opportunities.description:
# Medium: provider smoke-test script with reproducible fixtures

## Context
Lyrics providers can drift or fail unexpectedly. A quick smoke test improves contributor confidence before deeper debugging.

## Goal
Provide a simple command that validates core provider flows against known fixtures.

## References
- Existing script: scripts/test_real_alignment.js
- Fixtures: testing_assets/

## Requirements
1. Script runs locally with one command.
2. Uses stable fixture inputs.
3. Prints clear pass/fail summary per provider.
4. Exits with non-zero code on failure.
5. Has short run instructions in doc
opportunities.llm_fit_note: [OK] Testing script contribution aligns with automation and tooling strengths, but not a core full-stack or React opportunity. | wins: ios_shipping, a11y_wcag, AI_automation_engineering

3. Priority hints and when to escalate
4. Issue quality checklist (repro steps, acceptance

A Code of Conduct is strongest when reporting channels are concrete and safe.

4. Ensure language is respectful, privacy-aware, and unambiguous.

opportunities.title: [OSS] LuvLyrics/LuvLyricsApp: [Good First Issue] Add parser test coverage for timestamp edge cases
opportunities.description:
# Good First Issue: parser test coverage for timestamp edge cases

## Context for contributors
Timestamp parsing is fundamental to synced lyric display. This issue is intentionally scoped for beginners who want to add quality tests with low risk.

## Goal
Add comprehensive tests for edge-case timestamp formats and malformed input handling.

## Start here
- Parser file: src/utils/timestampParser.ts
- Test config: jest.config.js

## Beginner execution plan
1. Identify exported parser functions and expected output shape.
2. Create/extend test file for parser.
3. Add clear arrange/act/assert style
opportunities.llm_fit_note: [OK] Testing task aligns with frontend/React experience but not a core focus area. | wins: rubios_perf, a11y_wcag, ios_shipping

opportunities.title: [OSS] LuvLyrics/LuvLyricsApp: [Feature] Add robust retry and backoff strategy across lyrics providers
opportunities.description:
# Robust retry and backoff strategy across lyrics providers

## Context
Lyrics fetching depends on multiple providers. Inconsistent timeout/retry behavior harms reliability and can create request storms.

## Goal
Create a unified network resilience layer for lyrics provider calls.

## References
- src/services/MultiSourceLyricsService.ts
- src/services/LrcLibService.ts
- src/services/GeniusService.ts
- related provider service modules

## Required behaviors
- Shared timeout constants.
- Bounded retries for transient failures.
- Exponential backoff with jitter.
- Unified error taxonomy for UI/s
opportunities.llm_fit_note: [OK] Relevant TypeScript and API integration experience, but not a core frontend or full-stack role. | wins: rubios_perf, fullstack_taskflow, a11y_wcag

opportunities.description:
# Harden database migrations with schema versioning and rollback safety

## Context
Current migration style relies on column existence checks. As project grows, deterministic versioned migrations are safer.

## Goal
Add explicit schema versioning and resilient migration execution.

## References
- src/database/db.ts
- src/database/db_migration.ts

## Implementation objectives
1. Introduce a schema version source of truth.
2. Encode migrations as ordered, idempotent steps.
3. Handle migration failure gracefully (no half-broken state).
4. Document migration authoring rules for contributors.

##
opportunities.llm_fit_note: [WEAK] Issue is a low-level database migration task in an OSS project, not aligned with candidate's React/iOS/full-stack focus.

opportunities.title: [OSS] LuvLyrics/LuvLyricsApp: [Security] Secrets hygiene and credential rotation hardening
opportunities.description:
# Secrets hygiene and credential rotation hardening

## Context
Open-source repositories need explicit safeguards to prevent leaked credentials and accidental secret commits.

## Goal
Establish strong repo-level security hygiene and contributor instructions.

## Relevant files
- .gitignore
- SECURITY.md
- CONTRIBUTING.md
- README.md

## Work breakdown
1. Audit tracked files for sensitive patterns.
2. Add/verify ignore rules for env/credential artifacts.
3. Add sample env guidance (.env.example if needed).
4. Add credential rotation and incident response notes.
5. Add optional pre-commit/check
opportunities.llm_fit_note: [WEAK] Security hardening task is outside core expertise and not aligned with frontend/iOS development focus.
